mission.title: kubeflow: Notebook culling not working with Istio and RBAC
mission.description: Notebook culling not working with Istio and RBAC. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**

We deployed Kubeflow with Istio and Istio RBAC (w/ OIDC) enabled and we were looking to use Notebook culling to cull idle notebooks after a period of time.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Notebook culling not working with Istio and RBAC
steps.description:
Hi 

This fixed #5128 according to the guidelines of this comment: https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5128#issuecomment-859814573 . I wasn't sure where to place the `AuthorizationPolicy`, I think it belongs in the `overlays/kubeflow` directory, as istio is only used in combination with
```yaml
2020-07-13T21:11:51.792Z	INFO	culler	Error talking to http://construction-starts.ns.svc.cluster.local/notebook/dsd/construction-starts/api/status	{"error": "Get http://construction-starts.dsd.svc.cluster.local/notebook/dsd/construction-starts/api/status: read tcp 172.21.XX.YY:36004->10.0.XX.YY:80: read: connection reset by peer"}
```

steps.title: Confirm Notebook culling not working with Istio and RBAC is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Hi 

This fixed #5128 according to the guidelines of this comment: https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5128#issuecomment-859814573 . I wasn't sure where to place the `AuthorizationPolicy`, I think it belongs in the `overlays/kubeflow` directory, as istio is only used in combination with kubeflow.
codeSnippets: 2020-07-13T21:11:51.792Z	INFO	culler	Error talking to http://construction-starts.ns.svc.cluster.local/notebook/dsd/construction-starts/api/status	{"error": "Get http://construction-starts.dsd.svc.cluster.local/notebook/dsd/construction-starts/api/status: read tcp 172.21.XX.YY:36004->10.0.XX.YY:80: read: connection reset by peer"}

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: notebook-controller-deployment
  namespace: kubeflow
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: manager
        env:
        - name: ENABLE_CULLING
          value: "true"
        - name: IDLE_TIME
          value: "1" # 1 minute for testing, use something else in production
        - name: CULLING_CHECK_PERIOD
          value: "1"

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: notebooks-controller-culling
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
  - from:
    - source:
        principals: ["cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/notebook-controller-service-account"]
    to:
    - operation:
        methods: ["GET"]
        paths: ["*/api/status"] # wildcard for the name of the notebook server

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
